meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement robust SaaS pricing and overcome the risks of no version control. A pro-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for speed.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking feature changes. By integrating a structured SaaS pricing model with automated versioning, you prevent 'feature creep' and billing discrepancies. Use Antigravity to scaffold your pricing logic and Claude Code to enforce versioned API contracts, ensuring your client projects remain scalable, auditable, and professionally version-controlled from day one.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of a client calling about a broken feature that was 'working yesterday' is the universal nightmare of the modern freelancer. Without version control, you aren't just shipping code; you're playing a high-stakes game of Jenga where one wrong move collapses your entire revenue stream. The chaos of unversioned SaaS pricing logic—where tiers change without history—is the fastest way to lose professional credibility.

But there is a better way. By treating your SaaS pricing infrastructure as a versioned product, you transform from a reactive coder into a strategic architect. This guide reveals how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a bulletproof pricing engine that handles versioning automatically, giving you the speed to ship faster and the peace of mind that your work is perfectly documented.

steps.title: Define the Versioned Schema
steps.description: Establish a JSON-based pricing schema that includes a 'version' key. This ensures that every pricing change is identifiable and revertible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust, versioned JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model. Include fields for tier names, price_usd, features (as an array), and a schema_version integer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing_schema.json using the defined structure'

steps.title: Implement Version-Aware Logic
steps.description: Write a service layer that parses the schema version and applies the corresponding logic, preventing breaking changes in the billing pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript service that imports the pricing schema and routes logic based on the schema_version field. Include error handling for deprecated versions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor pricing_service.ts to handle multi-version schema routing'

steps.title: Automate Version Snapshots
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create a pre-commit hook that generates a snapshot of your current pricing configuration whenever a change is detected.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a bash script that validates the pricing schema and appends a timestamped snapshot to a local 'pricing_history' directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create pre-commit hook for pricing_history snapshots'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Metadata Versioning
steps.description: Ensure your payment provider reflects your internal versioning by syncing the schema version to Stripe metadata.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe integration module that maps internal pricing versions to Stripe product metadata during checkout creation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update stripe_client.ts to include schema_version in metadata'

steps.title: Audit and Rollback Testing
steps.description: Verify your system by simulating a rollback to a previous version and ensuring the billing logic remains consistent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that simulates switching between pricing versions and asserts that the correct pricing logic is executed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run test suite for pricing_version_logic.test.ts'

pro_tips: Always store pricing configurations in a dedicated folder separate from your business logic to keep the dependency graph clean.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your pricing schemas; treat a price increase as a minor update and a feature set change as a major update.
Implement an 'audit log' table in your database that stores the exact schema version used at the time of a user's subscription creation.
Automate the generation of a README.md changelog using Claude Code whenever a pricing schema file is modified.
Never hardcode prices in your UI; always fetch the versioned configuration from your pricing service to ensure a single source of truth.
content.next_steps: Deploy your versioned pricing service to a staging environment and run a smoke test against your production Stripe dashboard. Once confirmed, integrate the versioning logs into your client's admin dashboard so they can track pricing history themselves.
content.conversion_block: Stop shipping unversioned, fragile code. Master the architecture that separates the top 1% of freelancers from the rest. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
